name: tidepredict
documentation:
# tidepredict

A Python module for forecasting tides.

## Description

I wrote this module as I wanted a pure Python solution written to work
with my weather station which use the [WeeWX](http:http://www.weewx.com) Python codebase. WeeWX currently
uses [XTide](https://flaterco.com/xtide/), which is a fine application, but currently only has updated
harmonics constituents for US locations. I wanted a tool that could easily 
generate new harmonic constituents from a set of tidal measurements for locations outside the US.

The module is essentially a wrapper on the [pytides](https://github.com/sam-cox/pytides) module and uses
this codebase essentially unmodified to generate the harmonic constituents.

In keeping with good standards I've tried to maintain the command line interface options and 
output options of XTide. At present there is much to do and this module currently only supports the plain and list modes for output.

Currently the possible locations are limited to the
University of Hawaii's Research Quality Dataset:
[https://uhslc.soest.hawaii.edu]. 

If you run the tool with the option `-harmgen` it will automatically pull down the most recent two years of hourly measurements from the server and generate the harmonic constituents for the location.
After running this once, the constituents are saved locally and you don't need to do this again; unless you want updated data.

## Installation

```
pip install tidepredict
```

## Usage

### Example 1
```
python tidepredict -l Lyttelton
```
Should produce a series of tide predictions similar to the following:
```
Tide forecast for Lyttelton, New Zealand
Latitude:-43.60 Longitude:172.72
All times in TZ: Pacific/Auckland
2019-09-14 2259  0.63 Low Tide
2019-09-15 0502  2.18 High Tide
2019-09-15 1112  0.68 Low Tide
2019-09-15 1721  2.22 High Tide
2019-09-15 2338  0.64 Low Tide
2019-09-16 0544  2.17 High Tide
2019-09-16 1152  0.71 Low Tide
2019-09-16 1804  2.20 High Tide
2019-09-17 0018  0.66 Low Tide
2019-09-17 0628  2.16 High Tide
2019-09-17 1234  0.74 Low Tide
2019-09-17 1849  2.17 High Tide
```
the default is for three days from the current date and time. However you can add start and end times and dates using the `-e` and `-b` options.

 ### Example 2
```
python tidepredict -l Lyttelton -b 2019-11-02 00:00 -e 2019-11-03 00:00
```
```
Tide forecast for Lyttelton, New Zealand
Latitude:-43.60 Longitude:172.72
All times in TZ: Pacific/Auckland
2019-11-02 0309  0.53 Low Tide
2019-11-02 0929  2.54 High Tide
2019-11-02 1548  0.59 Low Tide
2019-11-02 2154  2.34 High Tide
```

 ### Example 3
```
python tidepredict -l Lyttelton -harmgen
```
This will search the research quality dataset for the hourly sea level measurements and generate harmonics constituents for them.

### Example 4
```
python tidepredict -l Lyttelton -fp
```
Create a tide graph using Python pandas. This is saved to the user
home directory / .tidepredict / tidegraph.png

### Example 5
```
python tidepredict -l Lyttelton -fc
```
CSV output
```
Lyttelton,2020-09-02,2259,Pacific/Auckland, 0.56, Low Tide
Lyttelton,2020-09-03,0459,Pacific/Auckland, 2.25, High Tide
Lyttelton,2020-09-03,1112,Pacific/Auckland, 0.61, Low Tide
Lyttelton,2020-09-03,1720,Pacific/Auckland, 2.32, High Tide
Lyttelton,2020-09-03,2340,Pacific/Auckland, 0.58, Low Tide
Lyttelton,2020-09-04,0542,Pacific/Auckland, 2.22, High Tide
Lyttelton,2020-09-04,1155,Pacific/Auckland, 0.64, Low Tide
Lyttelton,2020-09-04,1804,Pacific/Auckland, 2.26, High Tide
Lyttelton,2020-09-05,0022,Pacific/Auckland, 0.61, Low Tide
Lyttelton,2020-09-05,0628,Pacific/Auckland, 2.19, High Tide
Lyttelton,2020-09-05,1238,Pacific/Auckland, 0.68, Low Tide
Lyttelton,2020-09-05,1849,Pacific/Auckland, 2.21, High Tide
```


## License
MIT

functions.name: tidepredict.astro.s2d
documentation: Most of this is based around Meeus's Astronomical Algorithms, since it
presents reasonably good approximations of all the quantities we require in a
clear fashion.  Reluctant to go all out and use VSOP87 unless it can be shown
to make a significant difference to the resulting accuracy of harmonic
analysis.
Convert a sexagesimal angle into decimal degrees

functions.name: tidepredict.astro.polynomial
documentation: Evaluate a polynomial at argument

functions.name: tidepredict.astro.d_polynomial
documentation: Evaluate the first derivative of a polynomial at argument

functions.name: tidepredict.astro.T
documentation: Meeus formula 11.1

functions.name: tidepredict.astro.JD
documentation: Meeus formula 7.1

functions.name: tidepredict.astro._I
documentation: Now follow some useful auxiliary values, we won't need their speed.
See notes on Table 6 in Schureman for I, nu, xi, nu', 2nu''

functions.name: tidepredict.astro._nup
documentation: Schureman equation 224
Can we be more precise than B "the solar coefficient" = 0.1681?

functions.name: tidepredict.astro._nupp
documentation: Schureman equation 232

modules.name: tidepredict.process_station_list
documentation:
module to grab the list of stations from the UN Hawaii server and check
if the user specified string can be matched to a station

documentation: Get bytes IO objects from each of the station list files

functions.name: tidepredict.process_station_list.create_station_dataframe
documentation:
Creates a pandas dataframe from the csv list of all stations
downloaded from the uhrqds.

functions.name: tidepredict.process_station_list.read_station_info_file
documentation:
Reads the station info json file which contains downloaded and 
processed harmonics model data for each station that has had
-harmgen run

variables.name: tidepredict.constituent._mu2
documentation: 2MS2

methods.name: tidepredict.constituent.BaseConstituent.__eq__
documentation: Consider two out of phase constituents which travel at the same speed to
be identical

modules.name: tidepredict.ftp_helpers
documentation: FTP helper functions

functions.name: tidepredict.ftp_helpers.get_byte_stream
documentation:
Returns a file byte stream retrieved from an FTP site"
Input: 
    ftpfile is a string representing an internet ftp location

functions.name: tidepredict.process_station_info.get_station_info
documentation: returns info gathered about the station from the QA file

functions.name: tidepredict.process_station_info.deg_2_decimal
documentation:
Converts degrees minutes and seconds into decimal degrees

The inputs are strings of the form
dd-mmH (lat) ddd-mmH (lon)
where:
dd is the decimal degrees
mm is the minutes
H is the hemisphere N/S/E/W

modules.name: tidepredict.constants
documentation: Constants used throughout the module

functions.name: tidepredict.constants.qa_ftp
documentation: urls to QA data

methods.name: tidepredict.tide.Tide.__init__
documentation:
Initialise a tidal model. Provide constituents, amplitudes and phases OR a model.
Arguments:
constituents -- list of constituents used in the model.
amplitudes -- list of amplitudes corresponding to constituents
phases -- list of phases corresponding to constituents
model -- an ndarray of type Tide.dtype representing the constituents, amplitudes and phases.
radians -- boolean representing whether phases are in radians (default False)

methods.name: tidepredict.tide.Tide._prepare
documentation:
Return constituent speed and equilibrium argument at a given time, and constituent node factors at given times.
Arguments:
constituents -- list of constituents to prepare
t0 -- time at which to evaluate speed and equilibrium argument for each constituent
t -- list of times at which to evaluate node factors for each constituent (default: t0)
radians -- whether to return the angular arguments in radians or degrees (default: True)

methods.name: tidepredict.tide.Tide.at
documentation:
Return the modelled tidal height at given times.
Arguments:
t -- array of times at which to evaluate the tidal height

methods.name: tidepredict.tide.Tide.highs
documentation:
Generator yielding only the high tides.
Arguments:
see Tide.extrema()

methods.name: tidepredict.tide.Tide.lows
documentation:
Generator yielding only the low tides.
Arguments:
see Tide.extrema()

methods.name: tidepredict.tide.Tide.form_number
documentation: Returns the model's form number, a helpful heuristic for classifying tides.

methods.name: tidepredict.tide.Tide.classify
documentation: Classify the tide according to its form number

methods.name: tidepredict.tide.Tide.extrema
documentation:
A generator for high and low tides.
Arguments:
t0 -- time after which extrema are sought
t1 -- optional time before which extrema are sought (if not given, the generator is infinite)
partition -- number of hours for which we consider the node factors to be constant (default: 2400.0)

methods.name: tidepredict.tide.Tide._hours
documentation:
Return the hourly offset(s) of a (list of) time from a given time.
Arguments:
t0 -- time from which offsets are sought
t -- times to find hourly offsets from t0.

methods.name: tidepredict.tide.Tide._partition
documentation:
Partition a sorted list of numbers (or in this case hours).
Arguments:
hours -- sorted ndarray of hours.
partition -- maximum partition length (default: 3600.0)

methods.name: tidepredict.tide.Tide._times
documentation:
Return a (list of) datetime(s) given an initial time and an (list of) hourly offset(s).
Arguments:
t0 -- initial time
hours -- hourly offsets from t0

methods.name: tidepredict.tide.Tide.normalize
documentation: Adapt self.model so that amplitudes are positive and phases are in [0,360) as per convention

methods.name: tidepredict.tide.Tide.decompose
documentation:
Return an instance of Tide which has been fitted to a series of tidal observations.
Arguments:
It is not necessary to provide t0 or interval if t is provided.
heights -- ndarray of tidal observation heights
t -- ndarray of tidal observation times
t0 -- datetime representing the time at which heights[0] was recorded
interval -- hourly interval between readings
constituents -- list of constituents to use in the fit (default: constituent.noaa)
initial -- optional Tide instance to use as first guess for least squares solver
n_period -- only include constituents which complete at least this many periods (default: 2)
callback -- optional function to be called at each iteration of the solver
full_output -- whether to return the output of scipy's leastsq solver (default: False)

modules.name: tidepredict.timefunc
documentation: Helper module for for local to UTC time conversions.

modules.name: tidepredict.processdata
documentation:
Tide Predictor

Generates tide predictions using the pytides module.

Convert University of Hawaii research quality datasets into tidal
harmonic constituents for making predictions on any location that data
exists for.

Datasets and background info available from:
ftp://ftp.soest.hawaii.edu/uhslc/rqds/

functions.name: tidepredict.processdata.get_data_url
documentation:
returns the data file url for the uhslc server for a specific
ocean area.

accepted inputs are one of the following locations:
pacific
indian
atlantic

functions.name: tidepredict.processdata.predict_plain
documentation:
Generates tide predictions similar to Xtide's plain mode.
startdate for prediction (Python datetime)
enddate:  enddate for prediction
tide: the tide model to use

functions.name: tidepredict.processdata.reconstruct_tide_model
documentation:
Method to reconstruct the tide model from the dictionary of all stations
input: tm_file, which is the open json file

functions.name: tidepredict.processdata.process_unhw_data
documentation:
Processes university of Hawaii data into a Python dictionary.

Inputs:
years: years of data (default most recent two years) as a list of 
       year ints
location: unhw code for the location to retrieve. Default: Lyttelton, NZ

Outputs:
Dictionary in the form of {DateTime:TideLevel}
timestamps are hourly.

functions.name: tidepredict.processdata.output_to_file
documentation:
Outputs the processed datadict to a textfile

Mainly used for debugging purposes

functions.name: tidepredict.processdata.fit_model
documentation: Fits harmonic model to tides using pytides

functions.name: tidepredict.processdata.output_html
documentation:
Dumps a html page of a calendar month of tide predictions
todo: generalise

functions.name: tidepredict.nodal_corrections.f_unity
documentation: The following functions take a dictionary of astronomical values (in degrees)
and return dimensionless scale factors for constituent amplitudes.

functions.name: tidepredict.nodal_corrections.f_Mm
documentation: Schureman equations 73, 65

functions.name: tidepredict.nodal_corrections.f_Mf
documentation: Schureman equations 74, 66

functions.name: tidepredict.nodal_corrections.f_O1
documentation: Schureman equations 75, 67

functions.name: tidepredict.nodal_corrections.f_J1
documentation: Schureman equations 76, 68

functions.name: tidepredict.nodal_corrections.f_OO1
documentation: Schureman equations 77, 69

functions.name: tidepredict.nodal_corrections.f_M2
documentation: Schureman equations 78, 70

functions.name: tidepredict.nodal_corrections.f_K1
documentation: Schureman equations 227, 226, 68
Should probably eventually include the derivations of the magic numbers (0.5023 etc).

functions.name: tidepredict.nodal_corrections.f_L2
documentation: Schureman equations 215, 213, 204
It can be (and has been) confirmed that the exponent for R_a reads 1/2 via Schureman Table 7

functions.name: tidepredict.nodal_corrections.f_K2
documentation: Schureman equations 235, 234, 71
Again, magic numbers

functions.name: tidepredict.nodal_corrections.f_M1
documentation: Schureman equations 206, 207, 195

functions.name: tidepredict.nodal_corrections.f_Modd
documentation: See e.g. Schureman equation 149

functions.name: tidepredict.nodal_corrections.u_zero
documentation: Node factors u, see Table 2 of Schureman.

functions.name: tidepredict.nodal_corrections.u_L2
documentation: Schureman 214

functions.name: tidepredict.nodal_corrections.u_M1
documentation: Schureman 202
